solomon_islands_coalition_instability.claim: Solomon Islands coalition governments are notoriously fluid, with frequent no-confidence motions and shifting alliances.

provenance.definition: Solomon Islands coalition governments are notoriously fluid, with frequent no-confidence motions and shifting alliances.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: In 2019, Prime Minister Manasseh Sogavare survived a no-confidence motion but later resigned amid coalition realignments.
cases.basis_quote: Solomon Islands Prime Minister Manasseh Sogavare survived a no-confidence vote in April 2019, but his coalition fractured, leading to his resignation in May 2019.

cases.description: In 2021, Prime Minister Manasseh Sogavare was ousted in a no-confidence vote, replaced by Jeremiah Manele.
cases.basis_quote: Solomon Islands Prime Minister Manasseh Sogavare was ousted in a no-confidence vote on November 24, 2021, and Jeremiah Manele was elected as his successor.

china_influence_on_solomon_islands_polit.claim: China's growing influence in the Solomon Islands affects political alignments, with pro-China and pro-Taiwan factions.

provenance.definition: China's growing influence in the Solomon Islands affects political alignments, with pro-China and pro-Taiwan factions.

cases.description: In 2019, the Solomon Islands switched diplomatic recognition from Taiwan to China, leading to political realignments.
cases.basis_quote: Solomon Islands severed ties with Taiwan and established diplomatic relations with China on September 16, 2019, a move that reshaped domestic politics.

matthew_wale.quantity: Matthew Wale is elected Prime Minister

provenance.definition: Matthew Wale, as a candidate, is formally elected by parliament and sworn in as Prime Minister.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Matthew Wale is the individual elected and sworn in.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Any other individual is elected, or no election occurs by deadline.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: In 2021, Jeremiah Manele was elected Prime Minister after a no-confidence vote ousted Manasseh Sogavare.
cases.basis_quote: Jeremiah Manele was elected Prime Minister on November 24, 2021, following the ouster of Manasseh Sogavare.

cases.description: In 2019, Manasseh Sogavare was re-elected Prime Minister after a no-confidence motion failed.
cases.basis_quote: Manasseh Sogavare was re-elected Prime Minister on May 15, 2019, after surviving a no-confidence vote.

outcome_reference_class.quantity: Matthew Wale is elected Prime Minister of Solomon Islands following the May 2026 no-confidence vote

provenance.definition: Matthew Wale is formally elected by parliament and sworn in as Prime Minister on or after May 7, 2026, and before August 1, 2026.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Official announcement or credible news report confirming Matthew Wale's election and swearing-in.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Any other individual elected, or no election by deadline.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: In 2019, Manasseh Sogavare was re-elected Prime Minister after a failed no-confidence motion.

parliament_of_solomon_islands.quorum: Majority of 50 MPs (26) must be present to elect Prime Minis
parliament_of_solomon_islands.consequence_of_nonparticipation: If no Prime Minister is elected by August 1, 2026, the Governor-General may dissolve parliament and call a general election.
